channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chardham Yatra Online Registration Commences Today
item.description: The online registration process for the Chardham Yatra, one of the most revered pilgrimages in India, has commenced today. Devotees planning to undertake this sacred journey to the four holy shrines—Y...

item.title: Karnataka government assists 50 stranded citizens from UAE to return safely via Delhi
item.description: On the morning of March 6, 2026, around 50 individuals from Karnataka, who had been stranded in the UAE due to the ongoing West Asia conflict, arrived safely at Delhi airport. They had boarded a fligh...

item.title: Nadia Nakai clarifies relationship rumours with Toss and discusses upcoming reality show
item.description: Rapper Nadia Nakai has addressed rumours circulating on social media regarding a potential romance with fellow musician Toss, following a Valentine’s Day video that went viral. Speaking to Sowetan, Na...

item.title: The Social and Political Impact of Unemployment and Insecure Work
item.description: The outlook for job seekers in the UK is deteriorating, with unemployment projected to rise to 5.3% this year due to weak economic growth and uncertainty for employers. While political debates often f...

item.title: Nigerian Shippers’ Council and Port Police Strengthen Collaboration to Improve Cargo Flow
item.description: The Nigerian Shippers’ Council (NSC) don dey push for better partnership with Port Police to make cargo movement smooth and reduce unnecessary holdup for cleared containers. During a visit by the Comm...

item.title: Complaint Filed Against Suntay for Alleged Violation of Safe Spaces Act
item.description: ABS-CBN reports that Suntay is currently facing a formal complaint under the Safe Spaces Act, which aims to protect individuals from harassment and discrimination in public and private spaces. Details...

item.title: NASA Updates Artemis Program: First Crewed Moon Landing Shifted to Artemis IV in 2028, New 2027 Orbital Test Mission Added
item.description: NASA, under new Administrator Jared Isaacman, has announced significant revisions to the Artemis lunar program to increase mission cadence, reduce risks, and maintain the goal of a U.S. crewed Moon la...

item.title: Toronto Studio Designs Eco-Friendly and Artistic Balloon Bouquets
item.description: Toronto-based studio Ballooms, led by founder Dylan Kwacz, is redefining the concept of balloon arrangements by creating eco-friendly, visually striking bouquets made entirely from biodegradable latex...

item.title: Three Australians aboard US submarine during attack on Iranian warship confirmed
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has confirmed that three Australian Defence Force personnel were on board a US Navy submarine involved in the torpedoing of an Iranian warship off the coast of Sri Lank...

item.title: Review: Sree Vishnu shines in a grounded yet flawed Telugu crime thriller
item.description: Mrithyunjay, a Telugu crime drama written and directed by Hussain Sha Kiran, stars Sree Vishnu as Jay, an aspiring crime reporter, and Reba Monica John as police officer Sita. The film, spanning 122 m...

item.title: Nigeria Police Replace Force PRO Benjamin Hundeyin After Six Months
item.description: Di Nigeria Police Force don remove dem Force Public Relations Officer, ACP Benjamin Hundeyin, just six months after e start di position. Di removal approval happen on Thursday, March 5, 2025. People n...

item.title: Ceiling Panels Collapse at Canberra City Police Station Due to Water Damage
item.description: Ceiling panels in the gym area of Canberra's City Police Station collapsed this week, reportedly due to water damage. The incident has drawn attention to ongoing infrastructure issues in the station, ...

item.title: Shabana Mahmood defends UK government plans for humane deportation of failed asylum seeker families including children
item.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has defended the Labour government's new asylum reforms, which include provisions for the forced deportation of children in families whose asylum claims have failed. In ...

item.title: Geopolitical Risks of U.S. and Israeli Actions Against Iran
item.description: The article analyzes the escalating geopolitical tensions caused by U.S. and Israeli actions in Iran, highlighting the potential regional and global consequences. The author argues that the Trump admi...

item.title: Kenyan Driver Awarded KSh 410k After Court Rules Dismissal Unfair
item.description: A Kenyan driver employed by Meridian Driving College successfully sued his former employer after being unfairly dismissed and having his salary withheld. The Employment and Labour Relations Court conf...

item.title: Iran's Succession Process After Khamenei's Death: Interim Council Formed and Potential Candidates Emerge Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: Following the assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28, 2026, in joint US-Israeli strikes, Iran initiated its second supreme leader transition in the Islamic Republic's history. An Inter...

item.title: Investigation launched after teenage girl allegedly tortured in Domel, Bannu
item.description: A disturbing video has surfaced from Domel tehsil in Bannu, showing a 15-year-old girl, Hamshda, allegedly being tortured by unidentified armed men. Local sources say the girl had adopted masculine cl...

item.title: Tanzania Reports Major Progress in Reducing Under-Five and Maternal Mortality Rates
item.description: Tanzania imefikia hatua kubwa sana katika kuokoa maisha ya watoto na akina mama. Under-five deaths zimepungua kutoka 67 kwa kila 1,000 live births mwaka 2016 hadi 43 kwa 1,000 mwaka 2022. Hii ilifaham...

item.title: Pentagon designates Anthropic as a supply chain risk, prompting legal challenge
item.description: The U.S. Pentagon has formally designated artificial intelligence company Anthropic and its AI chatbot Claude as a 'supply chain risk,' a move that could force government contractors to cease using th...

item.title: Harriet Harman criticises leak of UK National Security Council discussions on US-Iran war request
item.description: Harriet Harman has strongly criticised the leak of discussions from a recent UK National Security Council (NSC) meeting, calling it 'utterly dysfunctional and destructive'. Speaking on the Sky News 'E...
